image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'Consider the graph of f(x) shown above'
the truth of derivative/second-derivative statements cannot be determined from text alone
multi_step: solving requires interpreting the graph and mapping visual behavior to f'(x) or f''(x) sign statements
the choices compare different intervals (0<x<1, 1<x<2, 2<x<3), requiring interval-specific reasoning
multiple candidate statements must be checked, but the task is still a single multiple-choice selection rather than a long chain computation
verifiability: answer is a discrete multiple-choice statement and matches one listed option
registry_passed=true with image and answer present
the claim can in principle be checked against the graph by verifying concavity/increasing-decreasing behavior on the specified intervals
